steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Line $u$ has equation $y = 2x + 10$, so its slope is $2$. A line perpendicular to $u$ has slope equal to the negative reciprocal of $2$, which is $-\frac{1}{2}$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$-\frac{1}{12}$': the negative reciprocal of $2$ is not $-\frac{1}{12}$. ✗
  • Choice B — '$-\frac{1}{8}$': not the negative reciprocal of $2$. ✗
  • Choice C — '$-\frac{1}{2}$': $-\frac{1}{2}$ is exactly the negative reciprocal of $2$. ✓
  • Choice D — '$-\frac{1}{10}$': confuses the $y$-intercept $10$ with the slope. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
